title: Password Policy
content: All employees must adhere to the following password requirements for enterprise systems. Passwords must be a minimum of 14 characters and include at least one uppercase letter, one lowercase letter, one numeric digit, and one special character. Passwords expire every 90 days and the system enforces a history of the last 12 passwords to prevent reuse. Accounts are locked after 5 consecutive failed login attempts and require a helpdesk ticket via ServiceNow to unlock. Service accounts and API keys must use passwords of at least 24 characters, rotated every 60 days, and stored exclusively in the approved secrets manager (Azure Key Vault). Temporary passwords issued during onboarding expire after 24 hours and must be changed upon first login. Passwords must not contain the user's name, email prefix, or common dictionary words. The IT Security team conducts quarterly audits of password compliance across Azure AD and all integrated applications. Violations detected during audits are escalated to the employee's manager and must be remediated within 48 hours. Repeated non-compliance may result in disciplinary action as outlined in the Acceptable Use Policy (doc-005).

title: Multi-Factor Authentication Requirements
content: Multi-factor authentication (MFA) is mandatory for all employees, contractors, and third-party vendors accessing enterprise systems. MFA must be enabled on Azure AD for all user accounts within 72 hours of account provisioning. The approved MFA methods are: (1) Microsoft Authenticator app push notifications, (2) hardware FIDO2 security keys, and (3) time-based one-time passwords (TOTP) via approved authenticator apps. SMS-based authentication is not permitted due to SIM-swapping risks. Conditional access policies require MFA for all sign-ins from outside the corporate network, sign-ins from unrecognized devices, and access to high-sensitivity applications including the HR portal, financial systems, and source code repositories. Privileged accounts (Global Admin, Security Admin, Exchange Admin) must use phishing-resistant MFA methods exclusively — hardware security keys or Windows Hello for Business. MFA enrollment tokens expire after 48 hours; employees who miss the enrollment window must submit a request through the IT self-service portal. Exceptions require written approval from the CISO and are reviewed every 30 days. The Security Operations team monitors MFA bypass attempts and investigates anomalies within 4 hours of detection.

title: Data Classification Policy
content: All data created, processed, or stored by the organization must be classified into one of four tiers. Public data carries no restrictions and may be freely shared externally. Internal data is intended for employee use only and must not be shared outside the organization without manager approval. Confidential data includes customer records, financial reports, strategic plans, and employee personal information; it must be encrypted at rest using AES-256 and in transit using TLS 1.2 or higher. Restricted data encompasses trade secrets, authentication credentials, cryptographic keys, and regulated data subject to GDPR or HIPAA; it requires end-to-end encryption, access limited to named individuals, and audit logging of every access event. Data owners are responsible for assigning classification labels using Microsoft Purview Information Protection within 5 business days of data creation. Mislabeled data discovered during quarterly reviews must be reclassified within 72 hours. Sharing Confidential or Restricted data externally requires a signed NDA and approval through the Data Governance Board. All classification decisions must be documented in the data catalog maintained on Confluence. Violations may result in access revocation and escalation per the Incident Response Procedure (doc-004).

title: Incident Response Procedure
content: The Incident Response Procedure defines the steps for identifying, containing, eradicating, and recovering from security incidents. All employees must report suspected incidents immediately via the dedicated Slack channel #security-incidents or by calling the 24/7 Security Operations Center hotline. Upon receiving a report, the SOC team triages the incident within 30 minutes and assigns a severity level: Critical (active data breach or ransomware), High (unauthorized access to Confidential or Restricted data), Medium (policy violation with potential exposure), or Low (failed attack attempts). Critical incidents trigger a war-room response within 1 hour, led by the Incident Commander, with mandatory participation from Security, Infrastructure, Legal, and Communications teams. Containment actions must begin within 2 hours for Critical incidents and 8 hours for High severity. Evidence must be preserved following chain-of-custody protocols and logged in the incident tracking system (ServiceNow ITSM). Post-incident reviews are mandatory within 5 business days of resolution and documented in Confluence. Root cause analysis findings feed into the risk register, and remediation tasks are tracked in Jira with a maximum resolution timeline of 30 days. Regulatory notification requirements under GDPR mandate reporting to the supervisory authority within 72 hours of confirmed personal data breaches.

title: Acceptable Use Policy
content: This policy governs the acceptable use of all organization-owned IT resources, including laptops, mobile devices, network infrastructure, cloud services, and software licenses. Employees must use corporate devices and accounts exclusively for business purposes; limited personal use is permitted provided it does not violate any other policy, consume excessive bandwidth, or create legal liability. Installation of unauthorized software requires approval via a ServiceNow change request. The use of personal cloud storage services (e.g., personal Google Drive, Dropbox) for business data is strictly prohibited; all file storage must use approved platforms such as SharePoint Online or OneDrive for Business. Employees must not attempt to bypass network security controls, access systems beyond their authorization level, or share credentials with others. Internet usage is monitored through the secure web gateway, and access to categories including gambling, adult content, and known malicious sites is blocked. Violation of this policy is subject to progressive disciplinary action: first offense results in a written warning, second offense in a 5-day access suspension, and third offense may lead to termination. All employees must acknowledge this policy annually during the security awareness training completed via the learning management system.

title: Remote Work Policy
content: Eligible employees may work remotely up to 3 days per week, subject to manager approval and role suitability. Remote work arrangements must be formally requested through the HR portal and approved within 5 business days. Employees working remotely must maintain a dedicated workspace that meets ergonomic guidelines documented on the intranet. The organization provides a one-time $500 home office stipend for approved remote workers, covering items such as monitors, keyboards, and ergonomic chairs; receipts must be submitted within 30 days of purchase through the expense system (Concur). Remote employees must be available during core business hours of 10:00 AM to 3:00 PM in their local time zone and responsive on Microsoft Teams within 15 minutes during these hours. All remote connections to corporate systems must use the approved VPN client (see VPN Setup Guide, doc-010) and comply with the Acceptable Use Policy (doc-005). Managers conduct quarterly check-ins to assess remote work effectiveness. Employees in client-facing roles may have additional in-office requirements as determined by their department head. Remote work privileges may be adjusted or revoked based on performance reviews or business needs with 14 days written notice.

title: Expense Reimbursement Policy
content: Employees may claim reimbursement for reasonable business expenses incurred in the course of their duties. All expense reports must be submitted through Concur within 30 calendar days of the expense date; late submissions require VP-level approval. Receipts are required for all expenses exceeding $25. Travel expenses must comply with the following limits: domestic airfare in economy class (business class requires director approval for flights over 6 hours), hotel accommodations up to $250 per night in standard markets and $350 per night in high-cost cities, and meals up to $75 per day inclusive of tips. Mileage for personal vehicle use is reimbursed at the current IRS standard rate of $0.67 per mile. Client entertainment expenses require pre-approval from the department head and must include the names and business purpose on the receipt. Software and subscription purchases up to $100 per month may be approved by direct managers; purchases above this threshold require IT procurement review. Expense reports are reviewed by the finance team within 10 business days, and approved reimbursements are processed in the next payroll cycle. Fraudulent claims are grounds for immediate termination and potential legal action.

title: Leave and Time-Off Policy
content: Full-time employees accrue paid time off (PTO) based on tenure: 15 days per year for 0-3 years of service, 20 days for 3-7 years, and 25 days for 7+ years. PTO accrues on a bi-weekly basis and may be carried over up to a maximum of 10 unused days into the following calendar year; excess days are forfeited on January 1. Employees receive 10 paid company holidays per year as published on the intranet by November 1 of the preceding year. Sick leave is provided at 10 days per year and does not carry over. Leave requests must be submitted through Workday at least 14 days in advance for planned absences; emergency leave requires notification to the manager within 2 hours of the scheduled start time. Parental leave provides 16 weeks of fully paid leave for primary caregivers and 6 weeks for secondary caregivers, available within 12 months of the birth or adoption. Bereavement leave of up to 5 days is granted for immediate family members. Jury duty and military leave are provided in accordance with applicable law. Extended unpaid leave of up to 90 days may be approved for personal reasons, subject to HR and departmental review.

title: Employee Onboarding Checklist
content: The onboarding process ensures new employees are provisioned, trained, and productive within their first 30 days. Prior to the start date, HR initiates account creation in Azure AD, assigns the employee to the appropriate security groups, and provisions a laptop through the IT asset management system (ServiceNow). On Day 1, the employee receives their device, completes identity verification, enrolls in multi-factor authentication (see MFA Requirements, doc-002), sets their initial password (see Password Policy, doc-001), and connects to the VPN (see VPN Setup Guide, doc-010). During Week 1, the employee completes mandatory training modules via the LMS: Security Awareness (2 hours), Data Classification (1 hour), Acceptable Use Policy (30 minutes), and GDPR Basics (1 hour). The employee's manager schedules a 30-60-90 day plan meeting by end of Day 3. Access to department-specific tools (Jira, Confluence, GitHub, Azure DevOps) is provisioned by the team lead within 48 hours of the start date. By Day 14, the employee should have completed all compliance training, attended the IT systems orientation, and joined relevant Teams channels. The 30-day onboarding review is conducted by the manager and HR to confirm all checklist items are complete and address any outstanding access or training gaps.

title: VPN Setup and Access Guide
content: All remote access to the corporate network must be established through the approved VPN solution (GlobalProtect by Palo Alto Networks). The VPN client is pre-installed on all corporate-managed devices; personal devices are not permitted to connect to the corporate VPN. To connect, employees launch the GlobalProtect client, authenticate using their Azure AD credentials, and complete the MFA challenge (see MFA Requirements, doc-002). The VPN supports split tunneling: corporate traffic is routed through the encrypted tunnel while general internet traffic uses the local connection. The VPN automatically disconnects after 12 hours of continuous use and requires re-authentication. Employees experiencing connection issues should first verify their internet connectivity, ensure the VPN client is updated to the latest version, and clear the client cache. Persistent issues should be reported via a ServiceNow ticket under the category 'Network — VPN Access' with a target resolution SLA of 4 hours during business hours and 8 hours outside business hours. VPN access logs are retained for 180 days and are subject to review by the Security Operations team. Contractors and third-party vendors receive VPN access through a dedicated gateway with restricted network segmentation, provisioned for the duration of their engagement plus a 7-day grace period.

title: Cloud Resource Provisioning
content: All cloud resources must be provisioned through the approved Infrastructure as Code (IaC) pipeline using Terraform modules maintained in the central GitHub repository. Direct provisioning via the Azure Portal or AWS Console is prohibited in production environments. Resource requests are submitted through a Jira ticket using the 'Cloud Provisioning' template, which requires: resource type, sizing justification, cost estimate, data classification level, and the owning team. The Cloud Engineering team reviews requests within 2 business days. Standard resource templates are available for common configurations: web application (App Service + SQL Database + Application Insights), microservice (AKS namespace + container registry), and data pipeline (Azure Data Factory + Storage Account + Synapse). All resources must include mandatory tags: environment, cost-center, owner-email, data-classification, and created-date. Resources without proper tags are flagged in the weekly compliance scan and auto-remediated after 14 days. Budget alerts are configured at 80% and 100% of the monthly allocation for each cost center. Production deployments require a completed change request in ServiceNow with a peer-reviewed Terraform plan. Non-production resources are automatically shut down nightly between 8:00 PM and 7:00 AM to reduce costs unless an exception is granted.

title: Disaster Recovery Plan
content: The Disaster Recovery (DR) plan ensures business continuity with defined recovery objectives: Recovery Time Objective (RTO) of 4 hours and Recovery Point Objective (RPO) of 1 hour for Tier 1 critical systems, and RTO of 24 hours and RPO of 4 hours for Tier 2 systems. Tier 1 systems include the customer-facing web application, payment processing platform, core database cluster, and authentication services. Tier 2 systems include internal collaboration tools, reporting dashboards, and development environments. The primary production environment is hosted in Azure East US 2, with a warm standby in Azure West US 2 that receives continuous data replication. Failover to the secondary region is triggered automatically when health probes detect 3 consecutive failures over 5 minutes, or manually by the on-call infrastructure engineer via the runbook in Azure DevOps. DR drills are conducted quarterly; full failover tests are performed semi-annually. Each drill is documented in Confluence with a post-mortem that evaluates actual vs. target RTO/RPO metrics. Backup integrity is verified weekly through automated restore tests to an isolated environment. The DR plan is reviewed and updated annually or after any significant infrastructure change. Key contacts, escalation paths, and vendor support numbers are maintained in the DR runbook, accessible offline via the printed emergency binder stored in each office.

title: Service Level Agreement Definitions
content: This document defines the internal Service Level Agreements (SLAs) for IT services provided to the organization. Tier 1 (Critical) services — including email, authentication, ERP, and customer-facing applications — guarantee 99.95% monthly uptime, measured as total minutes minus downtime divided by total minutes. Planned maintenance windows are excluded from SLA calculations and must be scheduled between 2:00 AM and 5:00 AM local time with 72 hours advance notice via email and the #it-announcements Slack channel. Tier 2 (Standard) services — including collaboration tools, internal dashboards, and development environments — guarantee 99.9% monthly uptime. Tier 3 (Best Effort) services — including sandbox environments and experimental tools — have no formal uptime guarantee. Incident response SLAs by severity: Critical incidents are acknowledged within 15 minutes and resolved within 4 hours; High within 30 minutes acknowledgment and 8 hours resolution; Medium within 2 hours acknowledgment and 3 business days resolution; Low within 1 business day acknowledgment and 10 business days resolution. SLA compliance is reported monthly in the IT Operations dashboard (Grafana) and reviewed in the monthly IT leadership meeting. Persistent SLA breaches trigger a formal improvement plan managed through Jira with executive sponsorship.

title: Data Retention Policy
content: This policy defines retention periods for all organizational data to ensure compliance with legal, regulatory, and business requirements. Financial records and tax documents must be retained for 7 years from the end of the fiscal year. Employee personnel records are retained for 5 years following the termination date. Customer transaction data is retained for 3 years after the last interaction; anonymized aggregate data may be retained indefinitely for analytics purposes. Email communications are retained for 2 years, after which they are automatically archived and deleted after an additional year unless subject to a legal hold. Application logs and audit trails are retained for 1 year in hot storage (Azure Log Analytics) and an additional 2 years in cold storage (Azure Blob Storage, Archive tier). System backups follow a grandfather-father-son rotation: daily backups retained for 30 days, weekly backups for 90 days, and monthly backups for 1 year. Data classified as Restricted (see Data Classification Policy, doc-003) must have its retention period explicitly defined in the data catalog and approved by the Data Governance Board. Upon reaching the end of its retention period, data must be securely deleted using methods that prevent recovery — cryptographic erasure for encrypted data or NIST 800-88 compliant sanitization for physical media. The Data Governance team conducts semi-annual retention compliance audits.

title: GDPR Compliance Guidelines
content: These guidelines ensure organizational compliance with the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) for processing personal data of EU/EEA residents. A Data Protection Officer (DPO) is appointed and serves as the primary contact for data subjects and supervisory authorities. All processing activities involving personal data must be recorded in the Record of Processing Activities (ROPA) maintained in Confluence and reviewed quarterly. Lawful bases for processing must be documented before any new data collection; consent must be freely given, specific, informed, and unambiguous. Data subjects have the right to access, rectification, erasure ('right to be forgotten'), restriction of processing, data portability, and objection. Subject access requests (SARs) must be fulfilled within 30 calendar days; complex requests may be extended by an additional 60 days with written notification to the requester. Data Protection Impact Assessments (DPIAs) are required before implementing new processing activities that pose high risk to individuals, including profiling, large-scale processing of sensitive data, or systematic monitoring. Personal data transfers outside the EEA require appropriate safeguards such as Standard Contractual Clauses or adequacy decisions. All employees complete annual GDPR training, and processing agreements with third-party vendors must include Article 28-compliant data processing addenda. Breaches involving personal data trigger the Incident Response Procedure (doc-004) with mandatory 72-hour supervisory authority notification.

title: Backup Procedures
content: This document defines the backup procedures for all organizational data and systems. Production databases are backed up continuously using point-in-time recovery with a 5-minute granularity window, enabled through Azure SQL automated backups. Full database snapshots are taken daily at 1:00 AM UTC. File storage on SharePoint Online and OneDrive for Business is protected by Microsoft's native versioning, retaining up to 500 versions per file. On-premises file servers are backed up nightly using the enterprise backup solution (Veeam) to an offsite location with AES-256 encryption in transit and at rest. Virtual machine snapshots are taken before every change window and retained for 7 days. Application configuration and Infrastructure as Code (Terraform state files, Kubernetes manifests) are version-controlled in GitHub with branch protection rules ensuring no direct commits to main. Backup integrity is validated through automated weekly restore tests executed in an isolated recovery environment; test results are logged in ServiceNow and reviewed monthly. Recovery procedures are documented in runbooks stored in Azure DevOps, with step-by-step instructions for each Tier 1 system. Backup failures trigger an immediate P2 alert to the on-call infrastructure engineer via PagerDuty, with a 2-hour resolution target. The Backup Procedures are reviewed semi-annually and updated to reflect changes in the infrastructure landscape.

title: Data Access Request Process
content: Access to organizational data beyond an employee's default role-based permissions must be requested through a formal process. Requests are submitted via the ServiceNow Data Access Request form, specifying: the data set or system, the business justification, the required access level (read-only or read-write), and the duration (temporary or permanent). All requests must be approved by the data owner and, for Confidential or Restricted data (see Data Classification Policy, doc-003), additionally by the Data Governance Board. Standard requests are processed within 3 business days; expedited requests for business-critical needs are processed within 4 hours with director-level approval. Access follows the principle of least privilege: users are granted the minimum permissions necessary to perform their role. Temporary access is automatically revoked at the end of the specified duration via automated Azure AD group membership expiry. Permanent access is reviewed quarterly during the User Access Review (UAR) cycle, where managers validate that each direct report's access remains appropriate. Unused access — defined as no login or query activity in 90 days — is flagged for revocation. All access grants, modifications, and revocations are logged in an immutable audit trail retained for 3 years. Third-party vendor access requires a signed data processing agreement and is provisioned with time-bound credentials expiring no later than the contract end date plus 24 hours.

title: Code Review Process
content: All code changes must be reviewed and approved before merging into the main branch. Pull requests (PRs) are submitted through GitHub and must include: a descriptive title, a summary of changes, linked Jira ticket(s), and evidence of passing automated tests. PRs require a minimum of 2 approving reviews from team members, with at least 1 reviewer being a senior engineer or tech lead. Reviews must be completed within 2 business days of submission; PRs without review after 3 days are escalated to the engineering manager. Reviewers evaluate code for correctness, readability, test coverage (minimum 80% line coverage for new code), security vulnerabilities (checked via SonarQube and Snyk), and adherence to the team's coding standards documented in Confluence. Draft PRs are encouraged for early feedback on architectural decisions. Comments must be actionable and constructive; 'nit' prefixed comments indicate suggestions that do not block approval. Authors must address or explicitly acknowledge all review comments before merging. Merge commits use squash-and-merge strategy to maintain a clean commit history. Hotfix PRs for production incidents may be approved by a single senior engineer and must be followed by a standard review within 24 hours. The team tracks review metrics monthly: median time to first review, median time to merge, and review comment density.

title: CI/CD Pipeline Standards
content: All applications must use the standardized CI/CD pipeline defined in the organization's GitHub Actions reusable workflow templates. The pipeline consists of four stages: Build, Test, Security Scan, and Deploy. The Build stage compiles source code, resolves dependencies from the approved artifact registry (Azure Artifacts), and produces a container image tagged with the Git SHA and semantic version. The Test stage executes unit tests (minimum 80% coverage gate), integration tests against ephemeral test environments, and contract tests for API consumers. The Security Scan stage runs static analysis (SonarQube), dependency vulnerability scanning (Snyk), container image scanning (Trivy), and secrets detection (GitLeaks). Any Critical or High severity findings block the pipeline and must be remediated before proceeding. The Deploy stage uses progressive delivery: changes deploy first to the development environment automatically on merge to main, to staging after automated smoke tests pass, and to production via a manual approval gate in GitHub Actions with a required sign-off from the release manager. Production deployments use blue-green deployment strategy with automated rollback triggered if error rates exceed 1% or p95 latency increases by more than 200ms within the first 15 minutes. Pipeline execution metrics are tracked in Grafana, with alerts for build failures and pipeline duration exceeding 20 minutes.

title: API Versioning and Release Management
content: All public and internal APIs must follow semantic versioning (MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH) and include the major version in the URL path (e.g., /api/v2/resources). Breaking changes — including removing fields, changing field types, or altering authentication mechanisms — require a major version increment and a minimum 6-month deprecation period for the previous version. Non-breaking additions (new optional fields, new endpoints) increment the minor version. Bug fixes increment the patch version. API documentation is auto-generated from OpenAPI 3.0 specifications and published to the developer portal (hosted on Azure API Management). All API changes require an updated OpenAPI spec committed alongside the code, reviewed as part of the standard code review process (doc-018). Release management follows a 2-week sprint cadence. Release candidates are tagged on Tuesdays, deployed to staging for validation Wednesday through Thursday, and promoted to production on the following Monday. Release notes are generated automatically from Jira tickets linked to merged PRs and published in Confluence. Emergency releases (hotfixes) bypass the sprint cycle but require a post-release review within 48 hours. Deprecated API versions display sunset headers indicating the retirement date and a link to the migration guide. Usage analytics for deprecated versions are monitored monthly; versions with fewer than 100 daily requests may be retired with 90 days notice to registered consumers.
